name: security-devsecops
description: Supply-chain + deployment-pipeline security: deps, SBOM, secrets rotation, CI/CD, IaC, container, pre-deploy gate. OMO-augmented with security-research mode + oracle + websearch + context7 + grep_app.

positive_triggers.input: I'm adding the new package 'lodash-utils' — should I be worried about anything?
positive_triggers.reason: Dependency addition triggers supply-chain security review (typosquatting check + npm audit)

positive_triggers.input: Our OAuth client secret hasn't been rotated in 18 months — is that a problem?
positive_triggers.reason: Secret rotation is explicit security-devsecops domain

positive_triggers.input: I just wrote a Terraform module that creates a public S3 bucket for our static assets
positive_triggers.reason: IaC + potentially-misconfigured public resource triggers IaC security lint

negative_triggers.input: Add a new REST endpoint that accepts user-uploaded files
I think we got breached — there are weird API calls in our logs
negative_triggers.reason: Post-breach detection / triage → security-incident-response, NOT devsecops

negative_triggers.input: Our p95 latency spiked 10x after last deploy
negative_triggers.reason: Post-deploy performance issue → observability-and-instrumentation or performance-optimization

behavioral_evals.scenario: User is preparing first production deploy of a Node.js app with a Dockerfile and Terraform manifest
expected_behavior: Agent runs dependency scan (npm audit --audit-level=high) — blocks merge on high/critical
Agent generates SBOM via syft / cdxgen, attaches to release artifact
Agent lints Terraform via tfsec / checkov — flags any 0.0.0.0/0 SG, public S3, * IAM
Agent scans container image via trivy image — flags any critical CVE in base image
Agent verifies secrets not in code (gitleaks detect), recommends secret manager if .env present
Agent verifies CI/CD has branch protection + OIDC + deploy key least-privilege
Agent asks Sisyphus to invoke /security-research mode for production-critical audit (3 hunters + 2 PoC)
Agent does NOT inspect application code (input validation / auth / OWASP) — that's security-and-hardening's domain

behavioral_evals.scenario: User adds a new dependency without running npm audit
expected_behavior: Agent identifies this as supply-chain risk (typosquatting, malicious package, vulnerability)
Agent checks package name against typosquatting patterns (e.g., 'lodash-utils' vs 'lodash')
Agent runs npm audit --audit-level=high (or higher) on the new dependency
Agent verifies the package is from a trusted registry (npmjs.com or company private registry)
Agent recommends npm ci in CI to prevent lockfile drift
Agent suggests adding npm audit to pre-merge check
Agent does NOT block on low-severity advisories (signal vs noise)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker. This skill complements security-and-hardening (app-layer) and security-incident-response (post-breach); see Related Skills in SKILL.md for routing chain.
